David understood that God’s displeasure was the cause of all the hardships he had experienced. And when God is working in our favor, it is good to remember our former troubles. Because their troubles began with God's displeasure, their prosperity must begin with his favor. Those divisions and conflicts that human foolishness and corruption create, only the wisdom and grace of God can repair, by pouring out a spirit of love and peace, by which alone a kingdom is saved from ruin. The anger of God against sin is the only cause of all misery, whether personal or public, that has been, is, or will be. In all these situations there is no solution except by returning to the Lord with repentance, faith, and prayer; asking him to return to us. Christ, the Son of David, is given as a banner to those who fear God; in him they are united, and find courage. In his name and strength they fight against the powers of darkness.

If Christ belongs to us, everything, one way or another, will work out for our eternal good. The person who is new in Christ may rejoice in all the precious promises that God has spoken in his holiness. Their present blessings and the sanctifying influence of the Spirit are certain guarantees of heavenly glory. David rejoices in conquering the neighboring nations that had been enemies to Israel. The people of God, through Christ, are more than conquerors. Even when they think that the Lord has abandoned them, he will bring them into the secure city eventually. Faith in the promise assures us that it is our Father’s good pleasure to give us the kingdom; but we are not yet made complete conquerors, and no true believer will misuse these truths to encourage laziness or vain confidence. Hope in God is the best foundation for true courage, because what need have those who have God on their side to fear? All of our victories come from him, and while those who willingly submit to our anointed King will share his glory, all of his enemies will be put under his feet.
